codeReview.summary: The fix directly addresses the prior blocking gap: commit f2f2a40 extends the existing ordinary_colon_strings_are_not_record_links regression so the same plain payload is now asserted across save(), get(), and Repo::get_record(), while raw storage still proves the value remains an ordinary string rather than re-entering record-link decoding. I found no remaining code issues within this fix feature's scoped objective.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The rust-library-worker skill tells workers to isolate their feature diff before commit, but in detached-head mission state this worker still inherited unrelated pre-staged scrutiny/library artifacts into the feature commit. The skill likely needs explicit instructions for how to proceed when the index is already dirty and safe isolation is impossible.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Worker handoff for session 35a38818-bed4-4fc4-894e-590285209e95 records followedProcedure=false and notes commit f2f2a40 included pre-staged .factory artifacts; git show --stat f2f2a40 shows the intended core/tests/integration_db.rs test change plus unrelated .factory review/library files.

summary: Pass. I reviewed the prior failure, the fix feature transcript skeleton and handoff, commit f2f2a40, and the updated regression in core/tests/integration_db.rs. The fix adds the missing get_record() proof for ordinary colon strings and adequately resolves the original scrutiny failure; the only follow-on observation is that the worker commit also captured unrelated pre-staged mission artifacts.
